name: glass
voice: frosted glass — light moving across a cool surface. Body words ride their own rounded backdrop-blur CHIPS on a stable lower-third rail (2-frame opacity, soft slide-up, a one-shot light-catch sheet; emphasis words take one diagonal specular glint). The apex assembles BEHIND the subject from flying frosted SHARDS that snap into a glass slab, the word fades on, one clean glint sweeps, the slab tilts and the edge light follows, then it shatters: cracks flash and the shards drop away.
when: premium product, design-tool, optics/camera and calm tech reveals — anything that wants a cool, glassy, restrained cinematic surface where the spectacle is refracted light, not impact. Elegant register. Needs a scene that tolerates a dusk dim + cool spill (any luma; brighter skies want the sky scrim it ships with).
